metadata.note: Output used as-is without manual revision or selection.

causal_texts: The glass slipped from Alice's hand. The glass fell to the floor. The glass broke into many pieces. Alice swept them up carefully.

noncausal_texts: A violin is played with a bow. A flute is played by blowing air. A drum is played by striking it. A harp is played by plucking strings.

A violin was played with a bow during small chamber concerts. A flute was played by steady breath during quiet garden recitals. A drum was played with firm strokes during lively street festivals. A harp was played with bare fingers during royal banquet music.

A trumpet was played with bright fanfares during public parade marches. A clarinet was played with reed tones during school orchestra rehearsals. A cello was played with deep notes during evening sonata programs. An oboe was played with clear phrases during formal civic ceremonies.

A banjo was played with quick patterns during country dance evenings. A trombone was played with sliding notes during brass ensemble shows. A mandolin was played with picks during outdoor folk gatherings. A bassoon was played with low passages during winter symphony nights.

A saxophone was played with smooth riffs during late jazz sessions. A tambourine was played with shaking rhythms during village wedding songs. A bagpipe was played with drones during highland field celebrations. A xylophone was played with mallets during cheerful children concerts.

A lute was played with plucked strings during court music lessons. A tuba was played with heavy tones during marching band drills. A recorder was played with soft melodies during classroom music hours. A sitar was played with resonant strings during cultural evening programs.

A mango was eaten fresh during humid summer afternoons on shaded porches. A pear was sliced thin for cool lunches beside garden benches. A plum was baked gently inside rustic tarts for autumn suppers. A pineapple was served chilled after spicy dinners in coastal homes.

A peach was eaten ripe during warm picnics near quiet rivers. A fig was paired with cheese at candlelit evening tables. A guava was blended smooth for breakfast drinks in market cafes. A cherry was scattered over desserts at family birthday meals.

A melon was shared cold during bright beach lunches in July. An apple was packed whole for long train rides across valleys. A papaya was served sweet beside yogurt bowls at sunrise. A grape was pressed softly for juice in vineyard kitchens.

A lime was squeezed fresh over grilled fish at harbor dinners. A banana was mashed smooth for early morning porridge bowls. An orange was peeled carefully during rainy station waits at dusk. A pomegranate was opened wide for festive winter platters at home.

A coconut was cracked open beside palm huts on hot afternoons. An apricot was dried slowly for mountain travel snacks. A blackberry was folded into cream during farmhouse dessert service. A kiwi was spooned cold from half shells after lunch.

A heron hunted fish beside shallow coastal lagoons during quiet mornings. A sparrow nested under roof tiles above busy market alleys. An owl watched fields from old barns during moonlit nights. A robin gathered worms across damp lawns after spring showers.

A crane rested near marsh reeds during long migration stops. A swallow circled church towers above narrow village streets at sunset. A falcon chased pigeons across windy cliffs during clear afternoons. A blackbird sang from hedge branches beside brick garden walls.

A pelican floated near harbor piers during calm sunny mornings. A finch pecked seeds around wooden feeders in snowy courtyards. A seagull followed trawlers beyond rocky beaches during stormy dawns. A woodpecker tapped trunks inside quiet pine forests all morning.

A canary fluttered inside bright cages near open bakery windows. A goose crossed farm ponds beside muddy grassy banks at noon. A parrot cracked nuts from metal bowls in tropical courtyards. A kingfisher flashed blue above narrow streams during early walks.

A raven perched on stone chimneys over windy hill farms. A stork stood motionless in wet meadows beside white fences. A dove settled near fountain edges within silent cloister gardens. A loon called across northern lakes during misty evening hours.

A hammer was used for framing work inside wooden houses. A chisel was used for carving details across oak cabinet doors. A wrench was used for tightening bolts on factory machinery. A shovel was used for turning soil in kitchen gardens.

A saw was used for trimming boards beside busy lumber yards. A plane was used for smoothing edges on maple furniture. A drill was used for boring holes through brick workshop walls. A rake was used for gathering leaves across park pathways.

A trowel was used for spreading plaster on interior walls. A level was used for checking shelves inside narrow storerooms. A clamp was used for holding joints during chair repairs. A hoe was used for clearing weeds between vegetable rows.

An axe was used for splitting logs near winter cabins. A file was used for shaping metal parts in garages. A mallet was used for setting tiles across stone courtyards. A sieve was used for sifting sand during masonry jobs.

A crowbar was used for lifting boards from old porches. A spade was used for edging paths around rose gardens. A pliers set was used for bending wire in workshops. A wheelbarrow was used for hauling bricks across building sites.

A tram carried commuters along crowded downtown tracks every weekday morning. A ferry carried bicycles across gray harbor waters before office hours. A tractor pulled wagons through muddy fields during harvest weeks. A scooter carried parcels across narrow alleys in old quarters.

A bus carried students past brick schools on rainy mornings. A wagon carried apples from orchards toward roadside produce stalls. A motorcycle carried riders along coastal roads during windy afternoons. A cable car carried visitors above steep hills near the bay.

A subway train carried shoppers beneath busy avenues on Saturdays. A snowmobile carried supplies across frozen trails during deep winter. A van carried tools between building sites across suburban roads. A canoe carried campers through wooded rivers during summer expeditions.
